pi_items.source: Task description requires 7 lifelines + 18+ message edges + activation boxes + combined fragments (alt/opt/loop). Total shape count of 20+ is appropriate for a comprehensive UML sequence diagram of this complexity.
Task description states 'at minimum 18 labeled arrows'. Analysis of task reveals exactly 18 distinct messages. Metadata threshold of 15 is actually more lenient than the explicit task requirement.
Task description explicitly requires: main sequence diagram page + second 'Threat Model' page listing 5 security threats

pi_items.metadata_value: user, browser, authorization server, token, resource, jwks, session
pi_items.verified_value: 7 participants: End User, Browser/SPA, Authorization Server, Token Endpoint, Resource Server, Session Store, JWKS Endpoint
pi_items.source: Task description lists exactly 7 lifelines with these names. Metadata participant list matches 1-to-1 with task requirements.
Task description specifies 'Save as ~/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.drawio'. Path expands to /home/ga/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.drawio for user 'ga'.
Task description specifies 'export as ~/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.png'. Path expands to /home/ga/Desktop/oauth2_sequence.png for user 'ga'.

pi_items.verified_value: RFC 6749 - The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework (IETF Standards Track, October 2012)
pi_items.source: Web search and IETF datatracker. Official URL: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749

pi_items.metadata_value: PKCE - Proof Key for Code Exchange
pi_items.verified_value: RFC 7636 - Proof Key for Code Exchange by OAuth Public Clients (IETF Standards Track, September 2015)
pi_items.source: Web search and IETF datatracker. Official URL: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636. Mitigates authorization code interception attack for public clients.

pi_items.verified_value: RFC 9068 - JSON Web Token (JWT) Profile for OAuth 2.0 Access Tokens (IETF Standards Track, October 2021)
pi_items.source: Web search and IETF datatracker. Official URL: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9068/. Defines interoperable JWT format for OAuth 2.0 access tokens.

pi_items.verified_value: 5 threats listed in setup_task.sh reference document
pi_items.source: Setup script explicitly lists: (1) Authorization Code Interception, (2) CSRF on Redirect, (3) Token Leakage, (4) Refresh Token Theft, (5) JWT Algorithm Confusion

privileged_info_summary: This task creates a UML sequence diagram of OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow with PKCE for a single-page application, based on verified IETF standards: RFC 6749 (OAuth 2.0 Framework), RFC 7636 (PKCE), and RFC 9068 (JWT Access Tokens). The diagram requires 7 lifelines (End User, Browser/SPA, Authorization Server, Token Endpoint, Resource Server, Session Store, JWKS Endpoint), minimum 15 message edges (task explicitly requires 18), and 20+ shapes. A second threat model page must document 5 security threats: authorization code interception, CSRF, token leakage, refresh token theft, and JWT algorithm confusion. Files are saved to /home/ga/Desktop/ in both draw.io and PNG formats.
